author: runner-5
rationale: Input pin only (P2 P-9): resolves the 'PINNED-AT-INPUTS:f2.inputs' harness_manifest placeholder to the staged-bytes manifest sha printed by poc/modal/modal_f2.py at launch (modal_common convention as specified by P2 section 1.2: sha256 over the canonical JSON — sort_keys, compact separators, UTF-8 — of the {relpath: sha256-of-file-bytes} staged-bytes manifest dict that the coordinator computes locally and the container re-computes and asserts byte-identical before running anything, ERR_STAGING_MISMATCH fail-closed; the full 155-entry dict itself ships in every run's provenance-modal.json staged_sha256). CAPTURED 2026-07-08 from a modal_f2.py --mock launch (app ap-8Hycvw78mvb7B1ft8eekoS, results poc/f2/results-incoming/20260708-193628-modal, MOCK-HARNESS-COMPLETE rc=0 — the launch also end-to-end-validated the exact staging this sha names) and REPRODUCED independently from the local bytes. WHAT THE SHA COVERS (155 files): runner/f2_runner.py fceb63a64d41... (the frozen 13-arm harness with ALL real-path backends now built: kernel verifier + IF-C extraction; d-xif consumption via run_extraction_instrument_dxif with fail-closed pins; d-ext external-slice loading with fail-closed pins; real HFPRM backend for the prm-verifier arm, Skywork PRM at the amendment-3 revision, validated by poc/modal/modal_prm_smoke.py; int4 backend validated by poc/modal/modal_int4_smoke.py); runner/requirements.txt; modal/requirements-image.txt (the pinned image environment incl. bitsandbytes 0.46.1 + accelerate 1.6.0 — runner_constraints says the image is pinned in this manifest, so the env pin file is inside the hashed set); the poc/f0 flop-meter package; inputs/f2-manifest.json (RunSpec with all corpus/model/file pins incl. the d-xif and d-ext file-level sha256s and the recorded IF-1 fork outcome); and the full data/d-qa, data/d-xif, data/d-ext, data/kernel-v0, data/molecules-v0 corpora byte-by-byte (their kot-corpus-hash/1 digests are separately pinned by amendments 1, 5, 4 and the frozen record respectively - this manifest pins the very same bytes at file granularity). CONSISTENCY: the corpus digests recomputed at write time all match their amendment pins (tools/registry/corpus-pin.py --record f2). NOTE (one-shot pin discipline): the ops overlay forbids re-replacing a resolved pin; ANY change to a staged byte (runner, inputs, requirements-image.txt, any corpus file) before the final-phase run invalidates this value and requires a pre-run correction record, not a new ops amendment. The final-phase launch reprints the sha; the run is valid only if it equals this pin.
